codeReview.summary: Clean implementation of GET /tokenlist endpoint with in-memory caching, proper error handling, and thorough test coverage. The endpoint reads data/tokenlist.json from disk (or TOKENLIST_PATH env override), caches the parsed result in memory, and serves it as JSON. Error paths return 500 with descriptive messages. The worker also made a valuable architectural change: exporting handleRequest and adding an isMainModule() guard so integration tests use the real server handler instead of a hand-rolled stub. Five new tests cover success response, content-type, token field shape, cache semantics, and missing-file error handling. All validators passed (typecheck, lint, tests, runtime curl checks).

issues.description: Error response leaks filesystem paths to clients. When tokenlist.json is missing, the 500 response includes the full ENOENT error message containing the server-side file path (e.g., 'ENOENT: no such file or directory, open /Users/.../tokenlist.json'). This is consistent with other error handlers in the codebase but is worth noting as a minor information disclosure concern.

Module-level cache variables (cachedTokenList, cachedTokenListPath) have no invalidation mechanism beyond process restart or TOKENLIST_PATH change. Intentional per feature design but means tokenlist updates require a server restart. Acceptable for a file that changes rarely.

data/tokenlist.json is untracked in git (Droid-Shield blocked the commit due to false-positive secret detections on token addresses). The endpoint will 500 on a fresh clone without this file. Worker documented this as a discovered issue and suggested adjusting Droid-Shield allowlist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The TOKENLIST_PATH environment variable was introduced as an override for the tokenlist file path but is not documented in environment.md or env.example. Future workers may not know it exists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts:84 — getTokenListPath() reads process.env.TOKENLIST_PATH; .factory/library/environment.md and env.example do not mention it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: handleRequest was exported and isMainModule() guard added to enable integration tests to use the real server handler. This is a significant testability pattern that should be documented so future workers know they can import handleRequest directly for integration testing rather than creating stub handlers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts:840 — 'export async function handleRequest'; src/__tests__/server.integration.test.ts:6 — import { handleRequest } from '../server.js'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker deviated from skill step 4 (Playwright verification) for this API-only feature, using curl + integration tests instead. The deviation is justified — /tokenlist has no UI rendering to verify. The skill's suggestion to allow explicit curl + integration-test verification for API-only features is reasonable and would avoid unnecessary Playwright overhead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false, deviation: 'This feature is API-only (/tokenlist endpoint) with no UI behavior change; command-level verification provided direct coverage of required assertions.'

summary: The tokenlist-endpoint feature passes review. Implementation is clean: a new GET /tokenlist route in server.ts reads data/tokenlist.json with in-memory caching, serves application/json, and returns descriptive 500 errors when the file is missing. The worker also improved testability by exporting handleRequest with an isMainModule() guard, replacing the previous stub-based integration tests with real handler tests. Five new test cases provide good coverage of success, content-type, shape, caching, and error behaviors. All non-blocking issues (path leakage in errors, no cache TTL, untracked data file) are documented and consistent with existing codebase patterns. The only actionable shared-state gaps are documenting the TOKENLIST_PATH env var and the new handleRequest export pattern.
